about_us_title: Who We Are & Why We Do It
about_us_intro: We are a team of dairy farmers building open-source software for the agricultural sector. Because we use this system on our own farms, we know exactly what works, what goes wrong, and what needs improvement. We aren't a corporate company full of people who have never seen a cow in real life—and that is our greatest strength. Built by Farmers for farmers
about_us_opensource_text: Because it’s fun, useful, and worth sharing! This project is completely free and easy to download. With computer vision, we can build reliable event detection quickly and affordably. The best part? Everything stays fully local—your data never leaves your farm. All of this is driven by the joy of freedom and independence.
about_us_team_text: The biggest advantage of open source is that it allows us to leverage powerful software from other projects that commercial entities cannot freely use. For instance, our core computer vision is built on the Ultralytics vision frameworks, and our detection alert system is powered by AI Detector. Without these tools, this project wouldn’t be possible.
about_us_longevity_text: Because all the code is completely online, the project can theoretically continue forever. If one of us has to step back, another developer can take easily over. This isn't a project that will quietly die; it will be maintained for years to come. In the future, we expect and encourage more contributors to join
about_us_independence_text: You are responsible for your own setups. You install it, update it, and hang the cameras yourself. We designed it to be as easy as possible because we don't have the time to maintain individual farm systems or deal with invoicing. The software runs locally, so you stay fully in control. Data is not transmitted and stays on your Farm and can run without wifi
about_us_reality_text: Because it's open-source, we say: 'It is what it is.' We cannot promise that it will work perfectly 100% of the time, and features like calving detection still need to get smarter. We currently have an active group of around 35 farmers running the system. In this group, users help each other out, and even farmers in their 50s and 60s manage the setup perfectly fine.
about_us_contribute_text: Cowcatcher AI is built by farmers, for farmers. Whether you're already using it or just getting started, we invite you to join our Telegram community. Connect with fellow farmers, share ideas, stay up to date, and get support whenever you need it. Many user-suggested improvements are already live in the code.
about_us_conclusion: Ultimately, we are constantly updating our vision modals and software. If it isn't quite what you need right now, check back in a few months!
ai_detector_hero_subtitle: One software program for both Calving and Cowcatcher. Monitor your video streams, catch the action, and get instant alerts.
ai_detector_about_p1: At Cowcatcher AI, we train and develop the vision models. The AI Detector is the software layer that applies these models in practice—using their learned knowledge to perform accurate, real-world detections.
ai_detector_about_p2: The detector could also be used outside the Cowcatcher provided models and used for your own projects—for example, alerting for people, cars, tractors pulling up, etc.
ai_detector_image_desc: You can choose how you would like to receive images of detections. Options include the full image, a zoomed-in view around the detection (for example, for calving), or both. The AI detector automatically selects the best captured moment to send to you, helping you make the most informed decision.
ai_detector_video_desc: The detector can send a short video of the event. Using a special technique, it records a smooth video, regardless of your computer’s performance. This is useful for estrus detection, as it helps determine whether the cow is standing still or actively mounting (in heat).
ai_detector_feature_local_desc: Keep your data private. Object detection runs completely locally on your hardware using lightning-fast YOLO models. No cloud required for the core detection.
ai_detector_feature_network_desc: Access the intuitive WebApp dashboard from your mobile phone, tablet, or desktop. As long as you are connected to the same Wi-Fi network as the host computer, you have full control.
ai_detector_feature_custom_desc: Make it your own. Whether you're alerting for cows, people, or vehicles, the entire system is easily tweaked through a single config.json file.
ai_detector_step_1_desc: The detector connects to your RTSP streams or video files. A fast YOLO model scans every frame looking for specific objects.
ai_detector_step_2_desc: To filter out false alarms, you can optionally ask a Vision Language AI (like Gemini) a custom question about the footage (e.g., 'Is there really a person?').
ai_detector_step_3_desc: Confirmed detections can be saved to disk, posted to a webhook, or sent straight to your Telegram chat complete with images and video clips.
ai_detector_install_desc: Getting started takes minutes, not hours. Choose the setup that fits your environment.
ai_detector_install_win_desc: The recommended approach. Just download the .exe for your hardware, put it in a folder, double-click, and fill out the generated config.json.
ai_detector_install_docker_desc: Perfect for Edge computers, Linux, or auto-reboot setups. Use the provided docker-compose file to get the detector and web app running instantly.
calvingcatcher_subtitle: Open source project - 24/7 Smart Herd Surveillance
calvingcatcher_hero_description: By combining the CalvingCatcher vision model with the AI detector, we create an automated warning system that monitors cows in real-time to detect the specific stages of calving.
calvingcatcher_basics_monitoring: 24/7 Monitoring: The program continuously monitors a live camera stream directed at the maternity pen.
calvingcatcher_basics_ai: Artificial Intelligence: It uses AI to automatically recognize calving signs. Trained on the Ultralytics Yolo framework for speed and accuracy.
calvingcatcher_basics_alerts: Instant Alerts: Upon detection, an instant photo and video alert is sent directly to your phone via Telegram or Home Assistant.
calvingcatcher_basics_non_intrusive: Non-intrusive: Your cameras remain fully functional for live viewing; we simply use an additional stream.
calvingcatcher_basics_local: Local Processing: Everything runs locally on your own computer in the barn, house, or office.
calvingcatcher_smart_learned: Learned Behavior: Just as you recognize a calving cow, this model is trained by farmers using real-world barn data to spot the water bag, legs, and head.
calvingcatcher_smart_confidence: Confidence Score: The system assigns a score to each detection to indicate how certain it is of the calving stage.
calvingcatcher_practice_continuous: Continuous Monitoring: The camera films the cows all day.
calvingcatcher_practice_analysis: Smart Analysis: Every few seconds, the program analyzes the activity.
calvingcatcher_practice_detection: Detection: Upon a reliable detection of a calving stage, the system begins saving frames.
calvingcatcher_practice_perfect_shot: The Perfect Shot: Multiple seconds of footage are collected to capture the best possible moment.
calvingcatcher_practice_notification: Sending Notifications: You receive a "Calving Detected" notification with the clearest images on your phone.
calvingcatcher_control_title: Important considerations – You are in control
calvingcatcher_control_tool: A tool, not a replacement: AI can sometimes trigger a false alarm. You make the final decision.
calvingcatcher_control_id: Animal Identification: The system does not read ear tags. You must identify the specific cow in the photo yourself.
calvingcatcher_control_physical: Physical checks remain crucial: Always head to the barn to confirm the situation when every second counts.
calvingcatcher_control_eyes: An extra pair of eyes: See it as a digital assistant for the moments you aren't there. Your expertise is indispensable.
calvingcatcher_value_constant: You don't have to constantly watch the cameras yourself.
calvingcatcher_value_night: You won't miss a calving event, even in the middle of the night.
calvingcatcher_value_fast: Instant alerts allow you to act fast when every second counts.
calvingcatcher_value_precision: Track the exact progression of the birth from the first signs.
calvingcatcher_privacy_local: Local Hosting: Your data never leaves your farm. The program runs completely locally.
calvingcatcher_privacy_req: Requirements: The PC must be on and connected to the internet to send notifications.
calvingcatcher_privacy_platforms: Platforms: We use Telegram or Home Assistant for alerts.
calvingcatcher_privacy_storage: Storage: Images are kept on your own PC. Only the best images are forwarded.
calvingcatcher_privacy_improvement: Continuous Improvement: You can help train the program further.
calvingcatcher_needs_pc: A Computer: Low cost consumer hardware, preferably with an NVIDIA graphics card (GTX 1000-series or newer).
calvingcatcher_needs_camera: IP Camera: Connect your existing IP/wifi cameras via RTSP.
calvingcatcher_needs_switch: POE Switch: To provide power and data to wired cameras.
calvingcatcher_needs_cable: LAN Cable: For a stable connection.
calvingcatcher_provide_model: The Trained AI Model: The vision "engine" trained on real-world calving data.
calvingcatcher_provide_privacy: Privacy: The model contains no imagery from other farms.
calvingcatcher_provide_os: Open Source Software: A versatile AI detector downloaded automatically upon startup.
calvingcatcher_disclaimer_risk: Provided "as is" without warranty of any kind. It is open-source software (AGPL-3.0).
calvingcatcher_disclaimer_fair: Please be fair and direct users to the official GitHub page.
title: Welcome to CowCatcherAI
subtitle: Open-source AI for the agricultural sector
about_text: Why open-source? Because we want to advance the agricultural sector with shared knowledge and AI.
cowcatcher_hero_description: Combining the Cowcatcher vision model with the AI-detector creates an automated alert system that monitors cows to detect "mounting" behavior.
cowcatcher_basics_monitoring: 24/7 Monitoring: The program monitors a live camera feed pointed at the cows around the clock.
cowcatcher_basics_ai: Artificial Intelligence: It uses AI to automatically recognize when one cow mounts another, built on Ultralytics Yolo.
cowcatcher_basics_alerts: Instant Alerts: Immediately sends photo and video notifications to your phone via Telegram or Home Assistant.
cowcatcher_basics_non_intrusive: Non-Intrusive: Your cameras remain functional; we simply use an additional stream.
cowcatcher_basics_local: Local Processing: Everything runs locally on your own computer in the stable or office.
cowcatcher_smart_learned: Learned Behavior: This program has been trained to recognize heat behavior using thousands of images.
cowcatcher_smart_confidence: Confidence Score: The system assigns a reliability score to every detection.
cowcatcher_practice_continuous: Continuous Monitoring: The camera films the cows all day.
cowcatcher_practice_analysis: Smart Analysis: The program analyzes activity every few seconds.
cowcatcher_practice_detection: Detection: High certainty detections trigger image and video capture.
cowcatcher_practice_perfect_shot: The Perfect Shot: It collects several seconds of footage for the clearest view.
cowcatcher_practice_notification: Sending Notifications: You receive a "Heat Detected" alert with visual proof.
cowcatcher_control_title: Important Considerations – You Stay in Control
cowcatcher_control_tool: A Tool, Not a Replacement: AI can trigger false alarms; you make the final decision.
cowcatcher_control_id: Animal Identification: You must identify the specific cow from the photo yourself.
cowcatcher_control_physical: Physical Checks Remain Vital: Always check for mucus and physical signs when in doubt.
cowcatcher_control_eyes: An Extra Pair of Eyes: A digital assistant for night-time or when you are away.
cowcatcher_value_constant: No need to watch the cows constantly.
cowcatcher_value_night: Don't miss heat cycles at night.
cowcatcher_value_fast: Immediate alerts allow for fast action.
cowcatcher_value_precision: Identify the exact onset of heat for precision insemination.
cowcatcher_privacy_local: Local Hosting: Runs on your own computer without cloud connections.
cowcatcher_privacy_req: Requirements: Computer must stay on and connected to the internet for alerts.
cowcatcher_privacy_platforms: Platforms: Uses Telegram or Home Assistant for local setups.
cowcatcher_privacy_storage: Storage: Images are stored locally; only the best are sent to your phone.
cowcatcher_privacy_improvement: Continuous Improvement: Option to further train the program yourself.
cowcatcher_needs_pc: A Computer: Ideally with an NVIDIA GPU (GTX 1000-series or newer).
cowcatcher_needs_camera: IP Camera: Most RTSP-capable cameras work (e.g., Reolink Trackmix).
cowcatcher_needs_switch: POE Switch: To power cameras via a single cable.
cowcatcher_provide_model: The Trained AI Model: The neural network 'brain'.
cowcatcher_provide_privacy: Privacy: Contains no images from other farms.
cowcatcher_provide_os: Open Source Software: A reusable third-party AI detector.
cowcatcher_disclaimer_risk: Use at your own risk. Open-source software under AGPL-3.0.
cowcatcher_disclaimer_fair: Please be fair and link to the official GitHub page.
docker_intro: This guide describes step by step how to install and configure an NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano for use with the AI-Detector (CowCatcherAI). All steps are fully detailed so that nothing is skipped.
docker_step1_opt1_title: Option 1: Booting from SD card or SSD (Basic Installation)
docker_step1_opt1_step1: Download the correct JetPack image via the NVIDIA website or AI-lab.
docker_step1_opt1_step2: Download and install BalenaEtcher on your PC.
docker_step1_opt1_step3: Insert the SD card into your computer.
docker_step1_opt1_step4: Flash the downloaded image to the SD card using BalenaEtcher.
docker_step1_opt1_step5: Insert the flashed SD card into the Jetson Orin Nano.
docker_step1_opt1_step6: Start the Jetson and complete the initial configuration.
docker_step1_opt1_note: Remember the chosen username and password well.
docker_step1_opt2_title: Option 2: Transferring boot from SD to SSD
docker_step1_opt2_step1: Connect a compatible SSD to the Jetson.
docker_step1_opt2_step2: Follow the steps in the video to boot the Jetson from the SSD instead of the SD card, so you can remove the SD card again.
docker_step1_opt3_title: Option 3: Installation via NVIDIA SDK Manager
docker_step1_opt3_step1: Install NVIDIA SDK Manager on a Linux host PC.
docker_step1_opt3_step2: Connect the Jetson via USB in recovery mode.
docker_step1_opt3_step3: Select Jetson Orin Nano and the desired JetPack version.
docker_step1_opt3_step4: Follow the installation procedure completely via SDK Manager.

download_intro_text: The installation is designed to be as easy as possible. Simply follow the steps for your operating system below and you can get started with CowCatcherAI right away.
download_card_windows_desc: For download on Windows 10 and Windows 11 computers.
download_card_macos_desc: For Apple devices such as the MacBook or other Mac computers.
download_card_linux_desc: For the Linux operating system and edge computers (such as the Jetson Nano Orin or Raspberry Pi). This installation is done via Docker.
footer_tagline: Your Barn, Your Data, Our Vision
footer_project_sub: We're a community-driven open-source movement revolutionizing AgTech. Built by farmers, for farmers
footer_contribute: Check the us out on GitHub
footer_made_with: Crafted with 💚 for the future of agriculture
front_page_subtitle: Your barn, Your data, Our vision
front_page_description: AI-powered open-source tools as your virtual assistant
front_page_cowcatcher_desc: A vision tool that detects mounting behavior a major indicator of cows in heat.
front_page_calvingcatcher_desc: A vision tool that detects key stages of calving, from the water bag and legs to the head and the newborn calf
front_page_ai_detector_desc: The local run tool for utilizing the vision models, with a lot of personal settings for tailering to your preferences
front_page_test_models_subtitle: Curious about what our YOLO models see? Try our interactive Hugging Face demos. Upload your own barn images or use our examples to see the AI in action.
front_page_stats_subtitle: Ensuring CowCatcherAI meets real farm needs through hands-on testing and field validation
front_page_stats_disclaimer: *We don't actually know exactly how many farms use the software since it's all locally run. This number only features the farms that actively contacted the project.
hardware_intro: For CowCatcherAI, you need two main hardware components: security cameras and a computer to process the images using AI.
hardware_cameras_text: You can use almost any IP camera, as long as it supports RTSP (Real-Time Streaming Protocol). This is a standard feature on most network cameras. Make sure the cameras are mounted high up in the barn for a clear, unobstructed view of the cows.
hardware_computer_text: The software runs locally on your own hardware for maximum privacy and speed. This can be a Windows/Linux PC with a capable graphics card (NVIDIA GPU is recommended for best performance), or a dedicated edge device like an NVIDIA Jetson Nano for a compact and energy-efficient setup in the barn.
install_info_text: You can easily do the hardware installation of the cameras and the computer yourself. The software is compatible with all camera brands. In the 'Hardware Installation' section, we explain everything in detail. If you already have cameras installed, you can download the software right away. Once that is successful, you can explore the possibilities of our software program in the 'Software Options' section.
install_card_hardware_desc: Read all about the required cameras, the computer, and how to install them correctly.
install_card_software_desc: Is your hardware ready? Download and install the CowCatcherAI software directly.
install_card_options_desc: Discover all the features and advanced settings of our software program.
jetson_text: Step 1: Flash the SD card with JetPack 4.6...
pc_text: Step 1: Install Python and the necessary CUDA libraries...
os_macos_intro: Easily start the detector on your Apple device.
os_macos_download_desc: Download the macOS file in the latest release.
os_macos_setup_step1: Create a folder, for example on your desktop or ~/aidetector, and move the downloaded file here.
os_setup_step2: Create a config.json file in the same folder (see Configuration documentation).
os_macos_setup_step3: Double-click the file or open it via the terminal — a terminal window will open with the detection logs.
os_setup_step3: Double-click the .exe — a terminal window will open with the detection logs.
os_setup_step4: Upon first startup (without an existing config.json), a template is generated automatically. Fill this in and restart the application.
os_tip_text: Keep the terminal window open as long as the detector is running. If it closes immediately, there is an error in your config.json — check carefully that you haven't forgotten any quotation marks (") or commas (,).
options_intro: The AI Detector is configured via a config.json file. Below is a complete and detailed overview of all available modules and variables.
opt_det_desc: Configure the sources the detector should monitor.
opt_det_source: RTSP/HTTP stream URL or local video path. Use a list [ ] for multiple sources.
opt_det_interval: Seconds to wait between processed frames (default 0). Useful to reduce load on slow machines.
opt_det_retention: How many recent frames to keep in memory per source (default 30) for context.
opt_yolo_desc: The fast first-pass AI that scans every frame. Without YOLO, all frames simply pass through.
opt_yolo_model: URL or local path to a YOLO model file (.pt or .onnx).
opt_yolo_conf: Confidence required (0-1). Can also be a per-class threshold, e.g., { "person": 0.85 }.
opt_yolo_timemax: Maximum duration in seconds to group frames into one event (default 60).
opt_yolo_timeout: Seconds of no detections before the event is considered over (default 5).
opt_yolo_cooldown: Seconds to wait before starting a new event. Prevents repeat alerts. Can be per class.
opt_yolo_trailing: Seconds of extra footage to include after the last detection (default 1).
opt_yolo_framesmin: How many frames in a row must match before counting (default 6, or 3 depending on GPU).
opt_yolo_imgsz: Image size fed into the model. Higher is more accurate but slower (default 640).
opt_yolo_strat: Which frames to evaluate: "LATEST" (most recent only) or "ALL".
opt_vlm_desc: A Vision Language Model checks the footage after a YOLO detection. Greatly reduces false alarms.
opt_vlm_prompt: The question to ask, e.g., 'Is there a person in this video?'.
opt_vlm_model: The AI model (e.g., 'gemini/gemini-2.0-flash'). Supports LiteLLM providers.
opt_vlm_key: API key for the chosen model provider.
opt_vlm_url: Custom API endpoint, useful for local models.
opt_vlm_strat: "VIDEO" sends a clip to the AI. "IMAGE" sends a single frame (cheaper but less accurate).
opt_exp_desc: Configure where confirmed detections should be sent.
opt_exp_disk_dir: Folder path under 'detections/' to save files into. Otherwise uses the class name.
opt_exp_disk_strat: "BEST" saves only the highest-confidence frame. "ALL" saves every frame.
opt_exp_disk_rej: Whether to also save detections rejected by the VLM (default true).
opt_exp_tele_chat: The Telegram chat or user ID.
opt_exp_tele_alert: Only send an alert every Nth detection (1 = every time, 5 = every 5th).
opt_exp_tele_plot: Include the full frame with a detection box drawn on it (default false).
opt_exp_tele_crop: Include a cropped image of just the detected object (default false).
opt_exp_tele_vid: Include an MP4 clip of the detection sequence (default true).
opt_exp_tele_width: Width of the video clip in pixels (default 1280).
opt_exp_tele_crf: Video quality (0-51). Lower = better quality, default 28.
opt_exp_tele_rej: Whether to also send detections rejected by the VLM (default false).
opt_exp_web_url: The URL to POST to when a detection occurs.
opt_exp_web_token: Authorization token sent in the request headers.
opt_exp_web_type: How image/video data is encoded in the payload: "binary" or "base64".
opt_exp_web_max: Maximum payload size in bytes. Images are compressed if they exceed this.
opt_exp_web_plot: Include the full frame with detection overlay (default false).
opt_exp_web_crop: Include a cropped image of the detected area (default true).
opt_exp_web_vid: Include an MP4 clip of the detection sequence (default false).
opt_exp_web_width: Width of the video clip in pixels (default 1280).
opt_exp_web_crf: Video quality (0-51). Lower = better quality, default 28.
opt_exp_web_rej: Whether to also POST detections rejected by the VLM (default false).
opt_health_desc: Sends a simple periodic HTTP request to verify the process is still alive.
opt_health_method: HTTP method: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, or HEAD (default GET).
opt_health_body: Optional request body sent as raw text.
opt_onnx_desc: Controls how ONNX Runtime configures before loading a YOLO model.
opt_onnx_winml: For Windows ML build only. Tries to register ML execution providers (default true).
opt_onnx_opset: ONNX opset used when exporting. Lower values can improve compatibility (default 20).
options_github_text: Bekijk de volledige documentatie, voorbeeldconfiguraties en de Docker setup op onze officiële GitHub pagina.
windows_intro: Option 1 — Windows Executable (recommended for most users). Start the detector quickly and easily on your Windows system.
windows_download_desc: Choose the correct file for your hardware in the latest release.
windows_hw_win10_rtx3000: Windows 10 with NVIDIA RTX 3000 series or newer
windows_hw_win10_rtx2000: Windows 10 with NVIDIA RTX 2000 series or older
windows_note_title: Not sure which one to choose?
windows_note_text: Start with the winml version on Windows. Only use a cuda build if you are certain that your NVIDIA setup matches that specific CUDA version.
windows_setup_step1: Create a folder, for example C:\aidetector, and move the downloaded .exe file to this folder.
